hero.title: Master Global Trade with Data-Driven Certainty.
hero.subtitle: Ensure compliance and optimize your supply chain before customs execution. From CBAM regulations to tariff optimization, we clear the path for your global expansion.
hero.trustline: Powering high-growth exporters and manufacturers worldwide.

problem.title: Breaking hidden barriers in global trade
problem.subtitle: International expansion often fails at the border. We eliminate the three most critical friction points before they impact your bottom line.

card1.title: Regulatory & Tariff Risks
card1.body: Incorrect HS codes and missing certifications lead to massive delays and penalties. Pre-auditing your data reduces operational friction by 40% and prevents costly post-clearance corrections.

card2.title: CBAM & ESG Accountability
card2.body: The Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) is no longer optional. We provide the data framework necessary to keep your products flowing through Europe's green corridors.

card3.title: Data-Driven, Not Intuition-Based
card3.body: Many firms choose new markets based on trends rather than logistics, tariff structures, and demand signals. Our analytics utilize weighted market modeling to your advantage.

methodology.title: From raw trade signals to board-ready strategy.
methodology.subtitle: DutyInsight's reports are not assembled — they are engineered. Every output flows through a structured intelligence pipeline that converts raw customs and market data into decisions you can defend in front of a board.

step1.title: Entry Intelligence
step1.body: Structured feasibility analysis for exporters and importers before any customs paperwork is touched. We model destination-market tariff exposure, regulatory load (CBAM, IOSS, ESG), and corridor logistics.
step1.deliverable: Pre-customs feasibility verdict with risk-mapped supplier or destination shortlist.

step2.title: Signal Synthesis
step2.body: Converting market signals into 'where and how' answers. Demand curves are read against bilateral trade flows and competitor positioning is mapped across origin geographies.
step2.deliverable: Quantified market prioritization with competitor and corridor matrices.

step3.title: Regulatory Forecasting
step3.body: Projecting the regulatory environment 18–36 months forward — CBAM phase-in, EU Customs Reform 2028 — and align the strategy to a calendar of compliance milestones.
step3.deliverable: Compliance roadmap with milestone calendar and exposure forecasts.

step4.title: Strategy Synthesis
step4.body: Converting intelligence into action through scenario modeling (controlled, balanced, accelerated) and decision architecture for leadership teams.
step4.deliverable: Board-ready strategy report with scenario comparison and decision triggers.

caseStudies.title: Intelligence in Action
caseStudies.subtitle: How DutyInsight's methodology resolves complex trade friction for global market leaders.
caseStudies.footnote: Confidentiality is paramount. Client names and identifying details have been redacted, while outcomes reflect verified performance metrics.

scenarioA.title: Triple-Market Activation in One Quarter
scenarioA.challenge: A mid-sized manufacturer based in the Aegean region stalled twice on regulatory gaps and tariff ambiguity in Central Europe.
scenarioA.approach: We deployed Export Entry Intelligence across Czechia, Poland, and Hungary, mapping every HS-level tariff and CBAM requirement while building a sector-specific visibility playbook calibrated to professional buyer behavior.
scenarioA.outcome: First commercial shipments completed within 12 weeks with zero documentation rejections. Management reported a structured expansion program.

scenarioB.title: Strategic Landed Cost Reduction
scenarioB.challenge: A distributor operating between Asian suppliers and EU buyers faced rising landed-cost volatility and existential CBAM threats.
scenarioB.approach: Conducted a 40-SKU classification audit and built a diversification model across 4 alternative sourcing geographies, weighted on tariff, logistics, and certification readiness.
scenarioB.outcome: Corrected HS coding reduced aggregate duty exposure; supplier diversification roadmap was adopted for pilot procurement with documented milestones.

scenarioC.title: Bespoke Regulatory Framework
scenarioC.challenge: Complex certification barrier intersecting with sanctions-screening rules where no standard analytical framework existed from other sources.
scenarioC.approach: Engineered a custom decision matrix integrating destination certification logic, sanctions-screening overlap, and origin-country compliance posture into a single reusable skill module.
scenarioC.outcome: Client adopted the framework's position and proceeded with disputed trade flows under documented analytical cover.

reports.title: Trade Intelligence Reports
reports.subtitle: Import Intelligence and Export Intelligence are offered in flexible packages (15, 25, or 50+) based on your catalog size. Our strategic analysis is a custom, one-time project for your firm.

strategy.title: Master the hidden dynamics of global trade.

feature1.title: Market Prioritization

feature2.title: Competitor Mapping
feature2.body: Detailed analysis of your competition's pricing strategies, supply chains, and market entry methods.

feature3.title: Regulatory Roadmap
feature3.body: Managing the impact of CBAM, ESG, and new EU/US trade regulations on your 2026-2028 growth cycle.

feature4.title: Board Briefing
feature4.body: Premium strategic reports (30-50 pages) and board-level presentations ready for investors.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: DutyInsight attaches the utmost importance to the protection of data belonging to the corporate clients who use our services. This Privacy Policy explains, in accordance with the Turkish Personal Data Protection Law No. 6698 (KVKK), the EU GDPR, and applicable legislation, the scope within which the data we collect through the dutyinsight.com website and platform is processed and protected.

section1.title: 1. Data Controller and Definitions
section1.body: DutyInsight acts as the data controller under both KVKK and GDPR regulations. Client refers to the legal entity benefiting from services; Personal Data means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person.

section2.title: 2. Categories of Data Processed
section2.body: The following categories of data are processed: Identification and Contact Data, Client Company Data (Tax ID, Trade Registry), Operational Trade Data (HS codes, trade routes), Technical Data (IP, logs), and Financial Data are processed.

section3.title: 3. Purposes and Legal Bases
section3.body: Data is processed for the Performance of a Contract, Legal Obligations, and Legitimate Interests (ensuring Platform security and service quality improvement).

section4.title: 4. Data Security
section4.body: The Company applies industry-standard technical and organizational measures (TLS 1.3, AES-256) to safeguard sensitive B2B data. Access is strictly restricted through RBAC and MFA.

section5.title: 5. Data Transfers
section5.body: Data may be shared with group companies, GDPR-compliant cloud processors, or public authorities where required by law. Cross-border transfers adhere to Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs).

section6.title: 6. Data Subject Rights
section6.body: As a data subject, you have the right to request access, rectification, deletion (right to be forgotten), and data portability under GDPR and KVKK protocols.

section7.title: 7. Retention Periods
section7.body: Data is retained only for the period required by law (e.g., 10 years for contracts, 5 years for financial records) or until the legal basis for processing expires.

section8.title: 8. Cookies
section8.body: Cookies are used for functional and analytical purposes. Please refer to our separate Cookie Policy for detailed management options.

section9.title: 9. Updates
section9.body: The Company reserves the right to revise this Policy. Material changes will be notified via email or platform notification.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For requests regarding your data, contact us at info@dutyinsight.com. Requests are addressed within the legal timeframe of 30 days.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms govern the use of dutyinsight.com and its intelligence services. By accessing the Platform, you accept these Terms as binding legal obligations.

section1.title: 1. Definition of Services
section1.body: Services include trade intelligence reports, market risk analyses, and CBAM/ESG assessments designed for professional B2B use.

section2.title: 2. Account Authorisation
section2.body: Clients must provide accurate and verifiable company information. Confidentiality of account credentials remains the sole responsibility of the Client.

section3.title: 3. Subscription and Usage
section3.body: Entitlements are valid for 12 months from the date of purchase. Digital B2B services are non-refundable once the report generation process has initiated.

disclaimer.title: 4. Legal Nature of Reports — DISCLAIMER
disclaimer.body: Reports constitute strategic advisory insights and do not replace official customs declarations or binding legal opinions. The Client remains solely responsible for any customs fines or administrative penalties.

liability.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
liability.body: Liability is limited to the amount paid for the Service in the 12 months preceding a claim. Indirect damages such as loss of profit or market share are strictly excluded.

section6.title: 6. Data Accuracy
section6.body: Reports rely on official third-party databases (TÜİK, Eurostat, UN Comtrade). The Company is not liable for errors originating from these institutional providers.

section7.title: 7. Client Obligations
section7.body: Information provided must be used for internal commercial activities only. Scraping, reverse engineering, or unauthorised access attempts are strictly prohibited.

ip.title: 8. Intellectual Property
ip.body: All proprietary content, algorithms, and methodologies remain the exclusive property of DutyInsight or its respective licensors.

section9.title: 9. Termination
section9.body: The Company reserves the right to suspend or terminate access for material breaches of these Terms or failure to fulfill payment obligations.

section10.title: 10. Force Majeure
section10.body: Obligations are suspended during events outside reasonable control (e.g., natural disasters, widespread cyber-attacks, or regulatory changes).

section11.title: 11. Governing Law
section11.body: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Türkiye. Disputes fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of the Istanbul (Çağlayan) Courts.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For contractual inquiries, contact us at info@dutyinsight.com.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This Policy explains the tracking technologies used on dutyinsight.com to provide a seamless professional experience. By using the Platform, you accept this usage.

section1.title: 1. What Is a Cookie?
section1.body: Cookies are small text files placed on your device to recognise you on subsequent visits and remember your language and session preferences.

section2.title: 2. Categories
section2.body: We utilize strictly necessary cookies for platform operations and analytics cookies for performance measurement.

essential.title: 3. Strictly Necessary
essential.body: These manage active sessions, security protocols, and language preferences. They are required for the platform to function and cannot be disabled.

analytics.title: 4. Analytics Cookies
analytics.body: These collect anonymised interaction data through GDPR-compliant tools to help us improve the user experience and reporting speed.

section5.title: 5. Third-Party Cookies
section5.body: We work only with verified providers (e.g., Vercel, Google Analytics) for technical performance monitoring and content delivery.

section6.title: 6. Management
section6.body: You can manage your cookie preferences through our on-site banner or your browser settings at any time.

section7.title: 7. Retention
section7.body: Persistent cookies are stored for a maximum of 13 months, after which your consent will be requested again.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For cookie-related questions, reach out to our DPO at info@dutyinsight.com.

cookieBanner.message: DutyInsight uses strictly necessary cookies to ensure the Platform functions correctly and analytics cookies to improve the user experience. You can manage your preferences or accept all cookies.
